title: polystichum anomalum subsp. travancoricum
intro: Polystichum anomalum subsp. travancoricum, also known as the Travancore shield fern, is a rare and endangered species of fern native to the Western Ghats of India. It is traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, known for its medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of the Travancore shield fern include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in decoctions, infusions, and powders to create remedies for various health conditions, and its leaves are also used in traditional rituals and ceremonies.
